body.raw:
# LightMode

In some occasions, you might want Chakra components to look the same in both light and dark modes. To achieve this, wrap the component in a `LightMode` or `DarkMode` component. Doing this will override the global `colorMode`.

## Import

```jsx live=false
import { LightMode } from '@node-real/bee';
```

## Usage

```jsx
function Example() {
  const { colorMode, toggleColorMode } = useColorMode();

  return (
    <HStack>
      <LightMode>
        <Tooltip content="In some occasions, you might want Chakra components to look the same in both light and dark modes">
          <Button size="sm" colorScheme="warning">
            Light Mode Always
          </Button>
        </Tooltip>
      </LightMode>

      <DarkMode>
        <Tooltip content="In some occasions, you might want Chakra components to look the same in both light and dark modes">
          <Button size="sm" colorScheme="danger">
            Dark Mode Always
          </Button>
        </Tooltip>
      </DarkMode>

      <Button size="sm" onClick={toggleColorMode}>
        Toggle Mode
      </Button>
    </HStack>
  );
}
```
